acceptance: Storage exposes verified bytes, in-flight bytes, flush state, hash state, and final completion truth as separate concepts.
notes: Verified and in-flight byte accounting are now separate storage concepts.

acceptance: HTTP/FTP/SFTP transfers write through StorageEngine and use verified completion truth.
verification: Full CTest passed after ordinary transfer progress switched to verified-only `completedLength`; restart-from-scratch RequestGroup coverage still passed.
notes: Range transfer semantics still use existing SegmentMan ownership.

acceptance: ED2K disk writes, AICH/MD4 validation, corrupt retry, and range cleanup use the modern storage boundary.
verification: Focused Ed2kCommand tests and ED2K transfer reclaim/parallel block tests passed after storage truth split.

acceptance: Control files and session state distinguish verified completion from in-flight progress and clean up only after safe finalization.
verification: DefaultProgressInfoFile tests passed. Control-file removal paths still depend on `allDownloadFinished()`, while save/load preserves in-flight pieces separately.
notes: This is the main non-BT 99 percent fix boundary.

acceptance: False-completion, resume, flush, hash failure, and control-file cleanup tests pass.
verification: `cmake --build --preset default`, full `ctest --preset default --output-on-failure`, CSV parser check, and `git diff --check` passed.
notes: Only high-value storage truth tests were added.
